fact: April 22 is exactly 112 days into the year - that's the same number as the emergency phone number in many countries!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for earthworms to go absolutely bonkers after spring rain, creating those squishy sidewalk obstacle courses.
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this date in most places on Earth - nature's perfect compass!
Dandelions are exploding into their full yellow glory right now in temperate regions, turning lawns into natural polka-dot carpets.
April 22 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, when the zodiac's most stubborn bull is in charge of the cosmic steering wheel.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere spots, leaves are starting their spectacular color-changing magic show as autumn kicks in.
Daylight is growing by about 2-3 minutes per day in northern places - that's like getting a free mini-episode of your favorite show every day!
This date sits exactly one month after the spring equinox, making it nature's official 'spring is really here now' announcement in the north.
Cherry blossom petals are typically raining down like pink snow in many temperate parts of the world around this time, creating the most beautiful natural confetti ever.
Frogs are usually in full croaking concert mode around this time in northern climates, turning ponds into nature's loudest karaoke bars.
The moon phase on April 22 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, the night sky becomes a perfect dark canvas for stargazing.
